get.description: Returns a list of D1 databases.

schema.description: a database name to search for.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters

post.description: Returns the created D1 database.

jurisdiction.description: Specify the location to restrict the D1 database to run and store data. If this option is present, the location hint is ignored.

name.description: D1 database name.

primary_location_hint.description: Specify the region to create the D1 primary, if available. If this option is omitted, the D1 will be created as close as possible to the current user.

200.description: Returns the created D1 database's metadata

get.description: Returns the specified D1 database.

patch.description: Updates partially the specified D1 database.

post.description:
Returns a URL where the SQL contents of your D1 can be downloaded. Note: this process may take
some time for larger DBs, during which your D1 will be unavailable to serve queries. To avoid
blocking your DB unnecessarily, an in-progress export must be continually polled or will automatically cancel.

current_bookmark.description: To poll an in-progress export, provide the current bookmark (returned by your first polling response)

no_data.description: Export only the table definitions, not their contents

no_schema.description: Export only each table's contents, not its definition

tables.description: Filter the export to just one or more tables. Passing an empty array is the same as not passing anything and means: export all tables.

output_format.description: Specifies that you will poll this endpoint until the export completes

at_bookmark.description: The current time-travel bookmark for your D1, used to poll for updates. Will not change for the duration of the export task.

error.description: Only present when status = 'error'. Contains the error message.

messages.description: Logs since the last time you polled

signed_url.description: The URL to download the exported SQL. Available for one hour.

200.description: Polled successfully, task no longer running (errored or complete)

at_bookmark.description: The current time-travel bookmark for your D1, used to poll for updates. Will not change for the duration of the export task.

messages.description: Logs since the last time you polled

202.description: Polled successfully, task is currently running

post.description:
Generates a temporary URL for uploading an SQL file to, then instructing the D1 to import it
and polling it for status updates. Imports block the D1 for their duration.

action.description: Indicates you have a new SQL file to upload.

etag.description: Required when action is 'init' or 'ingest'. An md5 hash of the file you're uploading. Used to check if it already exists, and validate its contents before ingesting.

action.description: Indicates you've finished uploading to tell the D1 to start consuming it

etag.description: An md5 hash of the file you're uploading. Used to check if it already exists, and validate its contents before ingesting.

filename.description: The filename you have successfully uploaded.

action.description: Indicates you've finished uploading to tell the D1 to start consuming it

current_bookmark.description: This identifies the currently-running import, checking its status.

at_bookmark.description: The current time-travel bookmark for your D1, used to poll for updates. Will not change for the duration of the import. Only returned if an import process is currently running or recently finished.

error.description: Only present when status = 'error'. Contains the error message that prevented the import from succeeding.

filename.description: Derived from the database ID and etag, to use in avoiding repeated uploads. Only returned when for the 'init' action.

messages.description: Logs since the last time you polled

final_bookmark.description: The time-travel bookmark if you need restore your D1 to directly after the import succeeded.

num_queries.description: The total number of queries that were executed during the import.

upload_url.description: The R2 presigned URL to use for uploading. Only returned when for the 'init' action.

200.description: Successful action. Import is either ready to start, under way, or finished (succeeded or failed).

at_bookmark.description: The current bookmark for your D1, used to poll for updates. Will not change for the duration of the import.

messages.description: Logs since the last time you polled

202.description: Polled successfully, task is currently running

post.summary: Import SQL into your D1 Database

post.description: Returns the query result as an object.
Returns the query result rows as arrays rather than objects. This is a performance-optimized version of the /query endpoint.

get.description:
Retrieves the current bookmark, or the nearest bookmark at or before a provided timestamp.
Bookmarks can be used with the restore endpoint to revert the database to a previous point in time.

parameters.description: An optional ISO 8601 timestamp. If provided, returns the nearest available bookmark at or before this timestamp. If omitted, returns the current bookmark.
parameters.name: timestamp

schema.description: An ISO 8601 timestamp used for time travel operations. The database will be restored to the nearest available bookmark at or before this timestamp.

bookmark.description: A bookmark representing a specific state of the database at a specific point in time.

post.description: Restores a D1 database to a previous point in time either via a bookmark or a timestamp.

parameters.description: A bookmark to restore the database to. Required if `timestamp` is not provided.
parameters.name: bookmark

schema.description: A bookmark representing a specific state of the database at a specific point in time.

parameters.description: An ISO 8601 timestamp to restore the database to. Required if `bookmark` is not provided.
parameters.name: timestamp

schema.description: An ISO 8601 timestamp used for time travel operations. The database will be restored to the nearest available bookmark at or before this timestamp.

post.summary: Restore D1 Database to a bookmark or point in time

Specifies the number of pipelines per page.

total_count.description: Indicates the total number of items.

success.description: Indicates whether the API call was successful.

get.description: Returns the queues owned by an account.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of queues

total_count.description: Total queues available without any search parameters

total_pages.description: Total pages available without any search parameters

200.description: List of all Queues that belong to this account

post.description: Create a new queue

get.description: Get details about a specific queue.

put.description: Updates a Queue. Note that this endpoint does not support partial updates. If successful, the Queue's configuration is overwritten with the supplied configuration.

get.description: Returns the consumers for a Queue

200.description: All consumers attached to this Queue

post.description: Creates a new consumer for a Queue

delete.description: Deletes the consumer for a queue.

get.description: Fetches the consumer for a queue by consumer id

put.description: Updates the consumer for a queue, or creates one if it does not exist.

post.description: Push a message to a Queue
Acknowledge + Retry messages from a Queue

lease_id.description: An ID that represents an "in-flight" message that has been pulled from a Queue. You must hold on to this ID and use it to acknowledge this message.

delay_seconds.description: The number of seconds to delay before making the message available for another attempt.

lease_id.description: An ID that represents an "in-flight" message that has been pulled from a Queue. You must hold on to this ID and use it to acknowledge this message.

ackCount.description: The number of messages that were succesfully acknowledged.

retryCount.description: The number of messages that were succesfully retried.

post.description: Push a batch of message to a Queue
Pull a batch of messages from a Queue

batch_size.description: The maximum number of messages to include in a batch.

visibility_timeout_ms.description: The number of milliseconds that a message is exclusively leased. After the timeout, the message becomes available for another attempt.

message_backlog_count.description: The number of unacknowledged messages in the queue

200.description: A batch of messages in the Queue

get.description: Get details about a Queue's purge status.

completed.description: Indicates if the last purge operation completed successfully.

started_at.description: Timestamp when the last purge operation started.

post.description: Deletes all messages from the Queue.

delete_messages_permanently.description: Confimation that all messages will be deleted permanently.

get.description:
Returns a list of R2 buckets that have been enabled as Apache Iceberg catalogs
for the specified account. Each catalog represents an R2 bucket configured
to store Iceberg metadata and data files.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific R2 catalog by bucket name.
Returns catalog status, maintenance configuration, and credential status.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

post.description:
Store authentication credentials for a catalog. These credentials are used
to authenticate with R2 storage when performing catalog operations.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

post.description:
Disable an R2 bucket as a catalog. This operation deactivates the catalog
but preserves existing metadata and data files. The catalog can be
re-enabled later.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description: Specifies the R2 bucket name to disable as catalog.
parameters.name: bucket_name

post.description:
Enable an R2 bucket as an Apache Iceberg catalog. This operation creates
the necessary catalog infrastructure and activates the bucket for storing
Iceberg metadata and data files.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description: Specifies the R2 bucket name to enable as catalog.
parameters.name: bucket_name

post.summary: Enable R2 bucket as a catalog

get.description:
Retrieve the maintenance configuration for a specific catalog,
including compaction settings and credential status.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

post.description:
Update the maintenance configuration for a catalog. This allows you to
enable or disable compaction and adjust target file sizes for optimization.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

get.description:
Returns a list of namespaces in the specified R2 catalog.
Supports hierarchical filtering and pagination for efficient traversal
of large namespace hierarchies.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description:
Opaque pagination token from a previous response.
Use this to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: page_token

parameters.description:
Maximum number of namespaces to return per page.
Defaults to 100, maximum 1000.
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description:
Parent namespace to filter by. Only returns direct children of this namespace.
For nested namespaces, use %1F as separator (e.g., "bronze%1Fanalytics").
Omit this parameter to list top-level namespaces.
parameters.name: parent

parameters.description:
Whether to include namespace UUIDs in the response.
Set to true to receive the namespace_uuids array.
parameters.name: return_uuids

parameters.description:
Whether to include additional metadata (timestamps).
When true, response includes created_at and updated_at arrays.
parameters.name: return_details

400.description: Bad request (e.g., invalid page_size, malformed parent namespace).

get.description:
Returns a list of tables in the specified namespace within an R2 catalog.
Supports pagination for efficient traversal of large table collections.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description:
The namespace identifier.
For nested namespaces, use %1F as separator (e.g., "bronze%1Fanalytics").
parameters.name: namespace

parameters.description:
Opaque pagination token from a previous response.
Use this to fetch the next page of results.
parameters.name: page_token

parameters.description:
Maximum number of tables to return per page.
Defaults to 100, maximum 1000.
parameters.name: page_size

parameters.description:
Whether to include table UUIDs in the response.
Set to true to receive the table_uuids array.
parameters.name: return_uuids

parameters.description:
Whether to include additional metadata (timestamps, locations).
When true, response includes created_at, updated_at, metadata_locations, and locations arrays.
parameters.name: return_details

400.description: Bad request (e.g., invalid page_size, malformed namespace).

get.description:
Retrieve the maintenance configuration for a specific table,
including compaction settings.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description: The namespace identifier (use %1F as separator for nested namespaces).
parameters.name: namespace

post.description:
Update the maintenance configuration for a specific table. This allows you to
enable or disable compaction and adjust target file sizes for optimization.

schema.description: Use this to identify the account.

parameters.description: The namespace identifier (use %1F as separator for nested namespaces).
parameters.name: namespace

get.description: Get Storage/Object Count Metrics across all buckets in your account. Note that Account-Level Metrics may not immediately reflect the latest data.

post.description: Creates temporary access credentials on a bucket that can be optionally scoped to prefixes or objects.

4XX.description: Create temporary access credentials response failure.

get.description: Returns the namespaces owned by an account.

schema.description: Maximum number of results per page.

post.description: Creates a namespace under the given title. A `400` is returned if the account already owns a namespace with this title. A namespace must be explicitly deleted to be replaced.

delete.description: Deletes the namespace corresponding to the given ID.

get.description: Get the namespace corresponding to the given ID.

delete.description: Remove multiple KV pairs from the namespace. Body should be an array of up to 10,000 keys to be removed.

4XX.description: Delete multiple key-value pairs response failure.

put.description: Write multiple keys and values at once. Body should be an array of up to 10,000 key-value pairs to be stored, along with optional expiration information. Existing values and expirations will be overwritten. If neither `expiration` nor `expiration_ttl` is specified, the key-value pair will never expire. If both are set, `expiration_ttl` is used and `expiration` is ignored. The entire request size must be 100 megabytes or less.

4XX.description: Write multiple key-value pairs response failure.

post.description: Remove multiple KV pairs from the namespace. Body should be an array of up to 10,000 keys to be removed.

4XX.description: Delete multiple key-value pairs response failure.

post.description: Retrieve up to 100 KV pairs from the namespace. Keys must contain text-based values. JSON values can optionally be parsed instead of being returned as a string value. Metadata can be included if `withMetadata` is true.

keys.description: Array of keys to retrieve (maximum of 100).

items.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid.

type.description: Whether to parse JSON values in the response.

withMetadata.description: Whether to include metadata in the response.

4XX.description: Get multiple key-value pairs response failure.

get.description: Lists a namespace's keys.

schema.description: Limits the number of keys returned in the response. The cursor attribute may be used to iterate over the next batch of keys if there are more than the limit.
Filters returned keys by a name prefix. Exact matches and any key names that begin with the prefix will be returned.

Opaque token indicating the position from which to continue when requesting the next set of records if the amount of list results was limited by the limit parameter. A valid value for the cursor can be obtained from the `cursors` object in the `result_info` structure.

4XX.description: List a Namespace's Keys response failure.

get.description: Returns the metadata associated with the given key in the given namespace. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name.

schema.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid. Use percent-encoding to define key names as part of a URL.

200.description: Read the metadata for a key response.

4XX.description: Read the metadata for a key response failure.

get.summary: Read the metadata for a key

delete.description: Remove a KV pair from the namespace. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name.

schema.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid. Use percent-encoding to define key names as part of a URL.

get.description: Returns the value associated with the given key in the given namespace. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name. If the KV-pair is set to expire at some point, the expiration time as measured in seconds since the UNIX epoch will be returned in the `expiration` response header.

schema.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid. Use percent-encoding to define key names as part of a URL.
A byte sequence to be stored, up to 25 MiB in length.

put.description: Write a value identified by a key. Use URL-encoding to use special characters (for example, `:`, `!`, `%`) in the key name. Body should be the value to be stored. If JSON metadata to be associated with the key/value pair is needed, use `multipart/form-data` content type for your PUT request (see dropdown below in `REQUEST BODY SCHEMA`). Existing values, expirations, and metadata will be overwritten. If neither `expiration` nor `expiration_ttl` is specified, the key-value pair will never expire. If both are set, `expiration_ttl` is used and `expiration` is ignored.

schema.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid. Use percent-encoding to define key names as part of a URL.
Expires the key at a certain time, measured in number of seconds since the UNIX epoch.
A byte sequence to be stored, up to 25 MiB in length.

4XX.description: Write key-value pair with metadata response failure.

ttlSeconds.description: How long the credentials will live for in seconds.

success.description: Indicates whether the API call was successful.

allowed.description: Object specifying allowed origins, methods and headers for this CORS rule.

headers.description: Specifies the value for the Access-Control-Allow-Headers header R2 sets when requesting objects in this bucket from a browser. Cross-origin requests that include custom headers (e.g. x-user-id) should specify these headers as AllowedHeaders.

methods.description: Specifies the value for the Access-Control-Allow-Methods header R2 sets when requesting objects in a bucket from a browser.

origins.description: Specifies the value for the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header R2 sets when requesting objects in a bucket from a browser.

exposeHeaders.description: Specifies the headers that can be exposed back, and accessed by, the JavaScript making the cross-origin request. If you need to access headers beyond the safelisted response headers, such as Content-Encoding or cf-cache-status, you must specify it here.

maxAgeSeconds.description: Specifies the amount of time (in seconds) browsers are allowed to cache CORS preflight responses. Browsers may limit this to 2 hours or less, even if the maximum value (86400) is specified.

destination.description: R2 bucket to copy objects to.

accessKeyId.description:
ID of a Cloudflare API token.
This is the value labelled "Access Key ID" when creating an API.
token from the [R2 dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:account/r2/api-tokens).

Sippy will use this token when writing objects to R2, so it is
best to scope this token to the bucket you're enabling Sippy for.

secretAccessKey.description:
Value of a Cloudflare API token.
This is the value labelled "Secret Access Key" when creating an API.
token from the [R2 dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:account/r2/api-tokens).

Sippy will use this token when writing objects to R2, so it is
best to scope this token to the bucket you're enabling Sippy for.

unsuccessful_keys.description: Name of the keys that failed to be fully updated. They should be retried.

changed_db.description: Denotes if the database has been altered in some way, like deleting rows.

changes.description: Rough indication of how many rows were modified by the query, as provided by SQLite's `sqlite3_total_changes()`.

duration.description: The duration of the SQL query execution inside the database. Does not include any network communication.

last_row_id.description: The row ID of the last inserted row in a table with an `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` as provided by SQLite. Tables created with `WITHOUT ROWID` do not populate this.

rows_read.description: Number of rows read during the SQL query execution, including indices (not all rows are necessarily returned).

rows_written.description: Number of rows written during the SQL query execution, including indices.

served_by_colo.description: The three letters airport code of the colo that handled the query.

served_by_primary.description: Denotes if the query has been handled by the database primary instance.

served_by_region.description: Region location hint of the database instance that handled the query.

size_after.description: Size of the database after the query committed, in bytes.

sql_duration_ms.description: The duration of the SQL query execution inside the database. Does not include any network communication.

lease_id.description: An ID that represents an "in-flight" message that has been pulled from a Queue. You must hold on to this ID and use it to acknowledge this message.

cursor.description: A continuation token that should be used to fetch the next page of results.

per_page.description: Maximum number of results on this page.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful

base64.description: Indicates whether or not the server should base64 decode the value before storing it. Useful for writing values that wouldn't otherwise be valid JSON strings, such as images.

expiration.description: Expires the key at a certain time, measured in number of seconds since the UNIX epoch.

expiration_ttl.description: Expires the key after a number of seconds. Must be at least 60.

key.description: A key's name. The name may be at most 512 bytes. All printable, non-whitespace characters are valid.

value.description: A UTF-8 encoded string to be stored, up to 25 MiB in length.

max_age.description: Specify the maximum duration (in seconds) items should persist in the cache. Defaults to 60 seconds if not specified.

stale_while_revalidate.description: Specify the number of seconds the cache may serve a stale response. Defaults to 15 seconds if not specified.

created_at.description: Indicates the creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

namespace_uuid.description: Contains the UUID that persists across renames.

updated_at.description: Shows the last update timestamp in ISO 8601 format. Null if never updated.

state.description: Specifies the state of maintenance operations.

target_size_mb.description: Sets the target file size for compaction in megabytes. Defaults to "128".

host.description: Defines the host (hostname or IP) of your origin database.

port.description: Defines the port of your origin database. Defaults to 5432 for PostgreSQL or 3306 for MySQL if not specified.

r2_lifecycle-date-condition.description: Condition for lifecycle transitions to apply on a specific date.

max_snapshot_age.description:
Specifies the maximum age for snapshots. The system deletes snapshots older than this age.
Format: <number><unit> where unit is d (days), h (hours), m (minutes), or s (seconds).
Examples: "7d" (7 days), "48h" (48 hours), "2880m" (2,880 minutes).
Defaults to "7d".

min_snapshots_to_keep.description: Specifies the minimum number of snapshots to retain. Defaults to 100.

state.description: Specifies the state of maintenance operations.

r2_lifecycle-age-condition.description: Condition for lifecycle transitions to apply after an object reaches an age in seconds.

r2_lock-rule-age-condition.description: Condition to apply a lock rule to an object for how long in seconds.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

delivery_delay.description: Number of seconds to delay delivery of all messages to consumers.

delivery_paused.description: Indicates if message delivery to consumers is currently paused.

message_retention_period.description: Number of seconds after which an unconsumed message will be delayed.

r2-data-catalog_api-response-errors.description: Contains errors if the API call was unsuccessful.

r2-data-catalog_table-identifier.description: Specifies a unique table identifier within a catalog.

name.description: Specifies the table name.

allOf.description: Specifies the state of maintenance operations.
Sets the target file size for compaction in megabytes. Defaults to "128".

target_size_mb.description: Updates the target file size optionally.

r2-data-catalog_table-compaction-config.description: Configures compaction settings for table optimization.

state.description: Specifies the state of maintenance operations.

target_size_mb.description: Sets the target file size for compaction in megabytes. Defaults to "128".

r2_class_based_metrics.description: Metrics based on what state they are in(uploaded or published).

created_at.description: Indicates the creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

location.description: Specifies the base S3 URI for table storage location.

metadata_location.description: Contains the S3 URI to table metadata file. Null for staged tables.

table_uuid.description: Contains the UUID that persists across renames.

updated_at.description: Shows the last update timestamp in ISO 8601 format. Null if never updated.

r2-data-catalog_namespace-identifier.description:
Specifies the hierarchical namespace parts as an array of strings.
For example, ["bronze", "analytics"] represents the namespace "bronze.analytics".

r2_object_size_metrics.description: Metrics on number of objects/amount of storage used.

payloadSize.description: Amount of storage used by object data.
